mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically raising present-value equity prices and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20-day forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following rate declines are not significantly higher than the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points relative to its trailing 12-month low, a Sahm-rule-style recession signal activates, causing investors to price in reduced corporate earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.6, near cycle lows, providing baseline for future rises
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to precede any NBER-dated recession within 6 months in the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signals broad-based consumer demand strength that jointly forecasts robust corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns; neither series alone is sufficient because retail strength without housing confirmation often reflects transitory spending rather than durable demand expansion.
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS+HOUST acceleration produces forward returns statistically indistinguishable from either single-series signal (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10 in walk-forward regression).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in industrial production (INDPRO) over the trailing 2 months reflects expanding real output capacity and corporate throughput, feeding directly into near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Correlation between INDPRO month-over-month change and next 5-day SPX return is not significantly positive (p > 0.10) across a 5-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized growth rate of nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) decelerates below 1% while UNRATE remains flat or rises, it signals an inflection in labor demand that historically precedes broad market drawdowns within the next month as earnings guidance is cut.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS deceleration triggers are positive in more than 55% of cases, or the average triggered return is not significantly below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: In a late tightening cycle where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, a simultaneous deceleration in CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth signals an approaching policy pivot; the joint occurrence of high rates and falling inflation causes markets to price in future easing, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter that would not occur from either series alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change decelerates by > 1pp
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.33 currently below trigger threshold, establishing baseline
failure_condition: In periods meeting the joint trigger, 60-day forward SPX returns are not significantly higher than in periods where only one condition holds (interaction coefficient p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above its trailing 2-year median and HOUST simultaneously declines more than 10% year-over-year, tight monetary policy is choking rate-sensitive housing demand, foreshadowing broader consumption weakness and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 10% YoY while FEDFUNDS is above its trailing 2-year median
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following triggered HOUST declines under tight policy are not significantly negative (p > 0.10), or reverse sign in more than 2 consecutive tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A stagflation signal forms when INDPRO declines on a 3-month basis while CPIAUCSL accelerates, indicating falling real output alongside rising input costs that compress corporate margins; this joint pattern produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month, whereas either signal alone (output decline with stable prices, or inflation with stable output) does not.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO 3-month change and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is not significant at p<0.10 in predicting 20-day forward returns, or single-series models explain equal or more variance than the joint model.
